wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the AutoPoV project overview section focused on introducing the autonomous proof-of-vulnerability framework. Explain its purpose as a hybrid agentic system combining static analysis (CodeQL, Joern) with AI-powered reasoning (LLMs via LangGraph) for vulnerability benchmarking. Document the key features including multi-source code ingestion, AI-powered detection, PoV generation, benchmarking capabilities, and multiple CWE support. Describe the target audience (security researchers, developers, security analysts) and primary use cases. Include architectural overview showing the relationship between backend API, frontend UI, agents, and external tools. Provide practical examples demonstrating the framework's value proposition in vulnerability research and automated security analysis. Use terminology consistent with the codebase and include both conceptual overviews for beginners and technical context for experienced security professionals.

wiki_catalogs.name: Agent-Based Workflow System
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for AutoPoV's agent-based workflow system focused on the LangGraph implementation and agent orchestration. Explain the AgentGraph class architecture including the state machine design with ScanState and VulnerabilityState data models. Document the five-node workflow: ingest_code, run_codeql, investigate, generate_pov, validate_pov, run_in_docker, and logging nodes. Detail the conditional edge logic for decision-making between PoV generation and skipping. Explain the agent factory pattern used for dynamic agent instantiation. Include the state transition diagram showing workflow progression from code ingestion through PoV execution. Document the integration points between specialized agents and their responsibilities. Provide examples of state mutations and error handling throughout the workflow. Address the fallback mechanisms when CodeQL is unavailable and LLM-only analysis is performed.

wiki_catalogs.name: System Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the AutoPoV system architecture section focused on the hybrid agentic framework design. Explain the layered architecture showing the relationship between FastAPI backend, React frontend, LangGraph agent system, and external tool integrations. Document the agent-based workflow orchestration using LangGraph, the pipeline pattern implementation, and component interactions. Include architectural diagrams illustrating data flow from code ingestion through static analysis to LLM reasoning and PoV execution. Describe the security architecture with Docker isolation, resource limiting, and network restrictions. Explain the technology stack integration including CodeQL, Joern, ChromaDB, and LLM providers. Address scalability considerations, deployment topology, and cross-cutting concerns like monitoring and logging.

wiki_catalogs.name: Environment Variables and System Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for AutoPoV environment variables and system configuration focused on environment variable setup and system initialization. Document all required environment variables including OPENROUTER_API_KEY, ADMIN_API_KEY, MODEL_MODE, MODEL_NAME, DOCKER_ENABLED, MAX_COST_USD, CODEQL_PATH, JOERN_PATH, VECTOR_STORE_PATH, and DATABASE_URL with detailed descriptions, data types, and recommended values. Explain the difference between development and production environment configurations. Detail system requirements including Python version, Docker availability, and hardware specifications. Document configuration validation and error handling mechanisms. Include environment variable precedence and override behavior. Provide complete configuration examples for different deployment scenarios including local development, testing, and production environments. Address common configuration mistakes and troubleshooting steps for environment-related issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Scan Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for AutoPoV's scan endpoints focused on the three primary scanning methods: Git repository scanning, ZIP file upload scanning, and direct code paste scanning. Document the POST /api/scan/git endpoint with parameters including url, token, branch, model, and cwes arrays. Detail the POST /api/scan/zip endpoint with multipart form data including file upload, model selection, and CWE filtering. Cover the POST /api/scan/paste endpoint with code content, language specification, and filename options. Include request/response schemas, authentication requirements, background task processing, and error handling patterns. Provide practical examples of curl commands and code snippets showing proper API usage. Document the scan status monitoring endpoint /api/scan/{scan_id} and server-sent events streaming endpoint /api/scan/{scan_id}/stream for real-time progress tracking.

wiki_catalogs.name: Agent Architecture Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for AutoPoV's agent architecture overview focused on the LangGraph-based workflow system. Explain the overall agent architecture including the AgentGraph class, state management with ScanState and VulnerabilityState dictionaries, and the workflow orchestration pattern. Document the state machine design with ScanStatus enumeration, node-based processing architecture, and conditional edge logic. Detail the integration points between agents and the centralized configuration system. Explain the state transition diagram showing the complete workflow from code ingestion through PoV execution. Include the factory pattern implementation for agent instantiation and the global agent graph singleton. Provide practical examples of state mutations and workflow execution patterns. Address error handling strategies, logging mechanisms, and progress tracking throughout the workflow.

wiki_catalogs.name: React Component Architecture
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive documentation for AutoPoV's React component architecture and routing system. Explain the component hierarchy starting from the root App component with React Router configuration. Document the routing structure including path definitions, route guards, and navigation patterns. Detail the component composition patterns, prop drilling strategies, and state sharing mechanisms. Explain the integration with Vite build tooling, CSS modules, and Tailwind CSS utility classes. Document component lifecycle management, error boundaries, and performance optimization techniques. Include examples of component imports, exports, and modular organization patterns. Address development server configuration, hot module replacement, and production build processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Static Analysis Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the static analysis integration component focusing on CodeQL query execution and Joern CPG analysis. Explain the CodeQL query architecture including BufferOverflow.ql, SqlInjection.ql, UseAfterFree.ql, and IntegerOverflow.ql with their specific detection patterns and logic. Document the query compilation process, execution pipeline, and result parsing. Detail the Joern CPG (Call Graph) analysis integration for program semantics understanding. Explain how static analysis results are parsed, normalized, and fed into the LLM reasoning pipeline. Include practical examples of query execution, result interpretation, and integration with the broader vulnerability detection workflow. Address performance optimization, query tuning, and troubleshooting common static analysis issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: PoV Script Generation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the PoV script generation process focused on automated exploit script creation. Explain the LLM-driven generation workflow including prompt formatting, CWE-specific template selection, and vulnerability context integration. Document the generation pipeline from vulnerability analysis to executable PoV script creation. Detail the prompt engineering approach for different CWE types including buffer overflow, SQL injection, integer overflow, and use-after-free scenarios. Explain the script cleaning and formatting process to remove markdown code blocks and ensure valid Python syntax. Include practical examples of generated PoV scripts for different vulnerability types. Address generation parameters, timing metrics, and error handling during script creation. Document the integration with the broader agent system and how generated scripts flow to validation and execution phases.

wiki_catalogs.name: Report Generation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for AutoPoV's report generation system focused on JSON and PDF report creation from scan results. Document the ReportGenerator class architecture including PDFReport custom class for PDF formatting. Explain report templates, styling options, and layout customization. Detail the JSON report schema structure including metadata, scan summary, metrics, and findings data. Document PDF report sections including executive summary, metrics tables, confirmed vulnerabilities details, and methodology documentation. Include practical examples of report generation workflows, custom styling options, and file output management. Address error handling for missing dependencies, report formatting limitations, and troubleshooting common generation issues. Provide guidance on integrating reports into automated workflows and CI/CD pipelines.

wiki_catalogs.name: Production Deployment
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for AutoPoV's production deployment focused on environment preparation, dependency management, and service configuration. Document step-by-step deployment procedures for production environments including system requirements, dependency installation, and configuration management. Explain environment variable setup, security configurations, and service initialization. Detail containerization options using Docker, including image building, container orchestration, and networking configuration. Include practical examples of deployment automation scripts, systemd service configuration, and reverse proxy setup. Address load balancing, SSL termination, and high availability configurations. Provide guidance for cloud deployment on AWS, GCP, or Azure including infrastructure provisioning and security groups.

wiki_catalogs.name: Docker Container Security
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for AutoPoV's Docker container security implementation focused on isolating and securing vulnerability execution environments. Document the container isolation mechanisms including network isolation with 'none' network mode, volume mounting with read-only permissions, and working directory restrictions. Explain resource limiting configurations including CPU quotas, memory limits, and execution timeouts. Detail the container lifecycle management including creation, execution, cleanup, and error handling. Document security best practices for container images, privilege escalation prevention, and file system isolation. Include practical examples of Docker security configurations, troubleshooting container security issues, and performance optimization for secure execution. Address container monitoring, logging security, and compliance considerations for production deployments.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Machine and Data Models
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for AutoPoV's state machine design focused on the ScanState and VulnerabilityState data models. Explain the TypedDict-based state definitions including scan_id, status, codebase_path, model_name, cwes array, findings list, current_finding_idx, timestamps, and cost tracking. Document the VulnerabilityState structure covering CVE ID, file path, line number, CWE type, code chunk, LLM verdict and explanation, confidence scores, PoV script and path, execution results, retry counts, inference time, cost estimation, and final status. Detail the ScanStatus enumeration values and their meanings. Explain the state mutation patterns throughout the workflow and how data flows between nodes. Include examples of state transitions and validation rules. Address the cost tracking mechanism and inference time calculations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the AutoPoV getting started section focused on helping users quickly set up and begin using the framework. Document prerequisites including Python 3.11+, Node.js 20+, Docker Desktop, and optional CodeQL CLI/Joern tools. Provide step-by-step installation instructions including repository cloning, virtual environment setup, dependency installation, frontend setup, and environment configuration. Include quick start tutorial with first scan walkthrough covering web UI usage, CLI commands, and API interactions. Document initial configuration steps including API key setup via CLI or web UI. Provide practical examples of common usage patterns including scanning Git repositories, local directories, and raw code. Address common setup issues and troubleshooting tips. Make content accessible to beginners while ensuring experienced developers can get productive quickly.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Flow and Processing Pipeline
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for AutoPoV's data flow and processing pipeline focused on the end-to-end vulnerability detection workflow. Document the complete data transformation pipeline from raw code ingestion through final PoV execution. Explain the state object evolution through each workflow stage including ScanState and VulnerabilityState modifications. Detail the data flow between external tools (CodeQL, LLMs) and internal processing components. Document the file processing pipeline including code chunking, vector store operations, and result aggregation. Explain the batch processing approach for multiple CWE types and the sequential handling of individual findings. Include examples of data structures at each pipeline stage and the transformation logic. Address error propagation and fallback mechanisms throughout the pipeline. Document the cost estimation and resource tracking integration.

wiki_catalogs.name: Core Features and Capabilities
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the AutoPoV core features section covering the complete feature set of the autonomous vulnerability detection framework. Document multi-source code ingestion capabilities including Git repositories, ZIP uploads, file/folder uploads, and raw code paste. Explain AI-powered detection using LLMs (GPT-4o, Claude, Llama3, Mixtral) for semantic vulnerability analysis. Detail the Proof-of-Vulnerability (PoV) system including automatic script generation and Docker-based execution. Cover benchmarking capabilities for comparing LLM performance on vulnerability detection metrics. Document web UI functionality with real-time scan progress, CLI tool for automation, and webhook integration for GitHub/GitLab. Include practical examples demonstrating each feature's value proposition and use cases for security researchers, developers, and security analysts.

wiki_catalogs.name: LLM Provider Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for AutoPoV LLM provider configuration focused on setting up and managing Large Language Model providers. Document the dual-mode LLM configuration supporting both online (OpenRouter) and offline (Ollama) providers. Explain MODEL_MODE options (online/offline) and their implications for performance and cost. Detail supported models for each provider including GPT-4, GPT-3.5-turbo, Claude-3, and local models like llama3 and mistral. Document API key management for OpenRouter including authentication setup and rate limiting considerations. Explain local model deployment with Ollama including model downloading, GPU acceleration, and resource allocation. Include model selection criteria based on vulnerability detection requirements and performance characteristics. Provide configuration examples for different use cases including research, development, and production environments. Address model switching procedures, fallback mechanisms, and troubleshooting connectivity issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Webhook Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for AutoPoV's webhook integration endpoints focused on GitHub and GitLab webhook handling. Document the POST /api/webhook/github endpoint with X-Hub-Signature-256 header validation, X-GitHub-Event header processing, and payload verification mechanisms. Detail the POST /api/webhook/gitlab endpoint with X-GitLab-Token header authentication, X-GitLab-Event header handling, and project access validation. Include webhook payload schemas, supported event types, security validation processes, and automatic scan triggering workflows. Provide examples of webhook setup configurations, payload samples, and integration patterns with CI/CD pipelines. Document error handling for invalid signatures, unsupported events, and malformed payloads. Address webhook retry mechanisms and debugging approaches for webhook delivery issues.

wiki_catalogs.name: Code Ingestion and Processing Agent
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the code ingestion and processing agent focused on codebase analysis and vector store integration. Explain the ingest_codebase agent implementation including code chunking algorithms, file processing workflows, and vector embedding creation. Document the agent's role in preparing code for LLM analysis by breaking down large codebases into manageable chunks with proper context preservation. Detail the integration with ChromaDB vector store, including embedding strategies, similarity search capabilities, and retrieval-augmented generation support. Explain file filtering logic, supported programming languages, and preprocessing steps. Include configuration options for chunk size, overlap, and embedding models. Provide practical examples of code ingestion workflows, error handling for malformed code, and performance optimization techniques. Address troubleshooting common ingestion issues and monitoring ingestion statistics.

wiki_catalogs.name: Page Components and Navigation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for AutoPoV's page-level React components and navigation system. Document each page component including Home, Results, History, Settings, ScanProgress, and Docs with their specific purposes and functionality. Explain the navigation patterns, route parameters, and data fetching strategies for each page. Detail the page lifecycle management, loading states, and error handling. Document the integration with backend APIs for data retrieval and real-time updates. Explain the responsive design considerations and mobile-first approach for each page. Include examples of page-specific state management, form handling, and user interaction patterns. Address SEO considerations, meta tags, and accessibility features for each page component.

wiki_catalogs.name: LLM-Based Vulnerability Analysis
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the LLM-based vulnerability analysis system focused on AI-powered security assessment. Explain the investigator agent architecture and its role in vulnerability validation using Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG). Document the prompt engineering strategy including INVESTIGATION_PROMPT, CODE_ANALYSIS_PROMPT, and RAG_CONTEXT_PROMPT with their specific parameters and context requirements. Detail the confidence scoring mechanism, verdict determination process, and false positive reduction techniques. Explain the multi-modal analysis combining static analysis results with LLM reasoning. Include practical examples of prompt formatting, context enhancement, and result interpretation. Address model selection criteria, cost optimization, and continuous improvement strategies for LLM-based detection accuracy.

wiki_catalogs.name: Validation and Quality Assurance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the PoV validation and quality assurance system focused on script verification and safety checks. Explain the multi-layered validation process including syntax validation using AST parsing, required print statement verification, standard library import restrictions, and CWE-specific validation rules. Document the LLM-based advanced validation that analyzes script logic and execution potential. Detail the issue detection system for common problems like missing vulnerability triggers, disallowed imports, and CWE mismatch scenarios. Explain the suggestion system that provides improvement recommendations for failing scripts. Include practical examples of validation results, common failure patterns, and resolution strategies. Address the will-trigger assessment mechanism and how validation results influence the PoV approval process. Document the retry analysis capability for failed attempts and how the system learns from validation feedback.

wiki_catalogs.name: Benchmarking and Performance Analysis
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed content for AutoPoV's benchmarking and performance analysis framework focused on comparative evaluation of LLM performance across vulnerability detection tasks. Document the analyse.py utility functions for processing scan results and generating comparative metrics. Explain benchmark methodologies including detection rates, false positive rates, and PoV success rates across different CWE types and datasets. Detail performance metrics calculation including cost analysis, inference time tracking, and resource utilization. Document comparative analysis workflows for evaluating different LLM models, configurations, and static analysis tools. Include practical examples of benchmark setup, result interpretation, and performance optimization recommendations. Address statistical analysis capabilities, trend identification, and research methodology support. Provide guidance for establishing baselines, tracking improvements, and conducting controlled experiments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Monitoring and Logging
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed content for AutoPoV's monitoring and logging systems focused on observability and operational visibility. Document monitoring strategies for API endpoints, agent workflows, and Docker execution environments. Explain logging configuration for FastAPI application, LangGraph agent states, and system events. Detail metrics collection including scan throughput, LLM token usage, Docker resource consumption, and error rates. Include practical examples of integrating with monitoring tools like Prometheus, Grafana, and ELK stack. Address alerting strategies for critical system failures, performance degradation, and security incidents. Provide guidance for log aggregation, retention policies, and compliance requirements. Document real-time monitoring dashboards and operational KPIs for system health assessment.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication and Access Control
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for AutoPoV's authentication and access control mechanisms focused on securing API endpoints and user management. Document the authentication system including API key generation, validation, and rotation processes. Explain authorization patterns for different user roles and permissions. Detail session management, token-based authentication, and security headers implementation. Document input validation and sanitization for authentication endpoints. Include practical examples of API key configuration, access control implementation, and security middleware setup. Address authentication best practices, credential storage security, and audit logging for authentication events. Provide guidance for integrating with external authentication systems and implementing multi-factor authentication.

wiki_catalogs.name: Workflow Orchestration and Node Implementation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for AutoPoV's workflow orchestration system focused on the five-node workflow implementation. Document the ingest_code node for codebase ingestion into vector store, run_codeql node for CodeQL analysis with fallback to LLM-only mode, investigate node for LLM-based vulnerability analysis, generate_pov node for PoV script generation, validate_pov node for quality assurance, and run_in_docker node for secure execution. Explain the sequential flow from code ingestion through PoV execution. Detail the state transition logic and error handling in each node. Document the integration with external tools like CodeQL CLI and vector stores. Include examples of node execution patterns, parameter passing, and result processing. Address the logging mechanism and progress tracking throughout the workflow.

wiki_catalogs.name: Security and Isolation Architecture

Explain the sandboxing approach for PoV execution with restricted permissions and environment variables. Detail the input validation and sanitization processes for code ingestion and user-provided data. Address the security considerations for webhook processing and external API integrations. Provide configuration examples for different security levels and deployment environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Supported CWE Types and Detection Methodology
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the supported CWE types section focused on the four primary vulnerability categories AutoPoV detects. Document CWE-119 (Buffer Overflow) with CodeQL query implementation and LLM-based pattern recognition. Explain CWE-89 (SQL Injection) detection through static analysis and dynamic exploitation patterns. Detail CWE-416 (Use After Free) vulnerability identification using memory access analysis. Cover CWE-190 (Integer Overflow) detection with arithmetic boundary checking. Describe the hybrid detection methodology combining CodeQL static analysis with LLM semantic reasoning. Include the detection pipeline showing how static analysis results are augmented with AI-powered validation. Document confidence scoring mechanisms and false positive reduction techniques. Provide examples of each CWE type with code snippets and detection patterns.

wiki_catalogs.name: Docker and Security Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for AutoPoV Docker and security configuration focused on containerized execution environment setup and security hardening. Document container cleanup procedures and resource management. Provide security audit guidelines and compliance considerations. Address Docker troubleshooting including permission issues, resource exhaustion, and container startup failures. Include production deployment security recommendations and monitoring setup.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication and Authorization Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for AutoPoV's authentication and authorization endpoints focused on API key management and security enforcement. Document the admin-only endpoints including POST /api/keys/generate for key creation, GET /api/keys for listing keys, and DELETE /api/keys/{key_id} for key revocation. Detail the API key verification process using the verify_api_key dependency and admin key validation through verify_admin_key. Include HTTP Bearer token authentication requirements, key format specifications, and security best practices. Document the admin key generation process, key lifecycle management, and access control patterns. Provide examples of API key usage in requests, key rotation procedures, and troubleshooting authentication failures. Address key storage security, rate limiting mechanisms, and audit logging for authentication events.

wiki_catalogs.name: PoV Generation and Verification Agent
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the PoV generation and verification agent focused on automated proof-of-vulnerability script creation and validation. Explain the agent's implementation of PoV generation using LLM reasoning, including script template selection, parameter injection, and syntax validation. Document the verification workflow including PoV script quality assessment, syntax checking, and logical consistency validation. Detail the agent's integration with CWE-specific templates, vulnerability pattern matching, and script generation strategies. Explain the validation process including static analysis of generated scripts, edge case handling, and retry mechanisms for failed validations. Include configuration options for generation parameters, validation thresholds, and template customization. Provide practical examples of PoV generation workflows, common validation failures, and troubleshooting techniques. Address script security considerations, input sanitization, and performance optimization for batch generation scenarios.

wiki_catalogs.name: State Management and Real-time Updates
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed documentation for AutoPoV's state management patterns and real-time update mechanisms. Explain the state management architecture including local component state, shared state patterns, and data synchronization with backend APIs. Document the Server-Sent Events (SSE) implementation for real-time scan progress updates and live logging. Detail the API client configuration, request/response handling, and error management strategies. Explain the state update patterns, optimistic updates, and conflict resolution. Document the integration with React hooks including useState, useEffect, and custom hooks for data fetching. Include examples of state initialization, update triggers, and cleanup procedures. Address performance considerations, memory management, and state persistence strategies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Confidence Scoring and Validation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the confidence scoring and validation system focused on vulnerability verification and quality assurance. Explain the multi-stage validation process including initial static analysis filtering, LLM-based reasoning, and PoV script generation validation. Document the confidence scoring algorithm with numerical ranges, threshold values, and decision criteria. Detail the validation mechanisms for reducing false positives including contextual analysis, pattern matching, and cross-referencing with static analysis results. Explain the retry mechanism for failed PoV attempts using RETRY_ANALYSIS_PROMPT and iterative improvement strategies. Include practical examples of confidence score interpretation, validation workflows, and quality metrics. Address performance considerations, accuracy optimization, and continuous validation improvement processes.

wiki_catalogs.name: Safety and Security Measures
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the safety and security measures implemented in the PoV generation and execution system. Explain the multi-layered security architecture including container isolation, resource limiting, and network restrictions. Document the timeout enforcement mechanisms, memory caps, and process monitoring to prevent system abuse. Detail the input validation and sanitization processes for PoV scripts before execution. Explain the standard library restrictions and import validation to prevent malicious code execution. Include practical examples of security configurations, threat mitigation strategies, and compliance measures. Address the logging and audit trail capabilities for tracking PoV execution activities. Document the fail-safe mechanisms and emergency shutdown procedures. Explain the security considerations for different execution environments and how the system adapts to various deployment scenarios. Include best practices for secure PoV generation and execution in production environments.

wiki_catalogs.name: Analytics Integration and Visualization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed content for AutoPoV's analytics integration and visualization capabilities focused on result storage, historical analysis, and dashboard integration. Document the results directory structure including runs and PoVs organization for long-term storage and analysis. Explain data persistence patterns, file naming conventions, and metadata management for historical tracking. Detail integration patterns with external analytics platforms, data warehouse systems, and visualization tools. Document historical analysis workflows including trend identification, regression detection, and performance monitoring. Include practical examples of dashboard creation, custom metric calculation, and automated reporting setup. Address data archiving strategies, retention policies, and backup procedures. Provide guidance on extending analytics capabilities, adding custom metrics, and integrating with existing security operations centers (SOC) workflows.

wiki_catalogs.name: Maintenance and Updates
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create detailed content for AutoPoV's maintenance and update procedures focused on system upkeep and evolution. Document update processes including dependency upgrades, security patches, and feature deployments. Explain backup strategies for configuration files, vector store data, and scan results. Detail maintenance procedures including database cleanup, log rotation, and temporary file management. Include practical examples of zero-downtime deployments, rollback procedures, and health check implementations. Address system monitoring for maintenance triggers including disk space alerts, memory pressure, and service availability. Provide guidance for automated maintenance tasks, scheduled cleanup jobs, and preventive maintenance schedules. Document upgrade paths for major version changes and breaking API modifications.

wiki_catalogs.name: LLM Security and Prompt Protection
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for AutoPoV's Large Language Model security measures focused on protecting against prompt injection attacks and ensuring responsible AI usage. Document prompt engineering security practices including input sanitization, context limiting, and instruction following enforcement. Explain guardrail implementation for preventing harmful content generation, bias mitigation, and ethical AI guidelines. Detail security measures for LLM interactions including rate limiting, input length restrictions, and output filtering. Document best practices for managing LLM credentials, API key security, and external service authentication. Include practical examples of secure prompt design, threat modeling for AI systems, and monitoring for suspicious LLM behavior. Address privacy considerations, data leakage prevention, and compliance requirements for AI-powered security analysis.

wiki_catalogs.name: Agent Factory Pattern and Dynamic Instantiation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop comprehensive content for AutoPoV's agent factory pattern implementation focused on the get_* functions for dynamic agent instantiation. Explain how the AgentGraph uses factory methods to instantiate specialized agents: get_code_ingester(), get_investigator(), get_verifier(), and get_docker_runner(). Document the benefits of this pattern for modularity and testability. Detail the agent lifecycle management and resource sharing between workflow nodes. Include examples of how agents are instantiated and configured within the workflow context. Explain the separation of concerns between the orchestrator and specialized agents. Address the configuration management and dependency injection through the factory pattern. Document the extensibility benefits for adding new agent types.

wiki_catalogs.name: Backend API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for AutoPoV's backend REST API focused on all public endpoints and their specifications. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication methods for each endpoint. Cover scan endpoints including /api/scan/git, /api/scan/zip, /api/scan/paste, and /api/scan/{scan_id} with parameter specifications and response formats. Document webhook endpoints for GitHub and GitLab integration including payload validation and security considerations. Detail report generation endpoints and file download capabilities. Include authentication requirements, API key management, and authorization patterns. Document error handling strategies, status codes, and common error scenarios. Provide practical examples of API usage with curl commands and code snippets. Address rate limiting, versioning information, and backwards compatibility. Include debugging tools and monitoring approaches for API consumption.

wiki_catalogs.name: Technology Stack Overview

Detail the backend stack including FastAPI for REST API development, LangChain/LangGraph for agent framework, and async programming patterns. Document the frontend stack with React, Vite for build tooling, and Tailwind CSS for styling. Explain the database and storage components including ChromaDB for vector storage and file system for results management. Cover external tool integrations including CodeQL CLI for static analysis, Joern for CPG generation, and Docker SDK for containerized execution. Document LLM provider integrations supporting both online (OpenRouter) and offline (Ollama) models. Include version requirements, compatibility considerations, and integration patterns between components.

wiki_catalogs.name: System and Utility Endpoints
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for AutoPoV's system and utility endpoints focused on health checks, metrics, and system information. wiki_items.title: Supported CWE Types and Detection Methodology
System Integration and External Tool Coordination
Agent Factory Pattern and Dynamic Instantiation
Sample Vulnerability Files and Educational Examples

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>

# AutoPoV: Autonomous Proof-of-Vulnerability Framework

## Project Introduction

AutoPoV is an advanced autonomous vulnerability detection and proof-of-vulnerability framework designed for LLM benchmarking and security research. The project combines cutting-edge AI technologies with traditional security analysis tools to automatically identify, investigate, and validate security vulnerabilities in codebases.

### Purpose Statement
AutoPoV serves as a comprehensive solution for automated vulnerability discovery and verification, enabling researchers and security professionals to benchmark Large Language Models against real-world security challenges while generating executable proof-of-vulnerability scripts.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Automated Vulnerability Detection**: Intelligently scan codebases for multiple vulnerability types including buffer overflows, SQL injection, use-after-free, and integer overflow
- **Proof-of-Vulnerability Generation**: Automatically create executable scripts that demonstrate security flaws
- **LLM Benchmarking**: Provide standardized testing ground for evaluating AI-powered security analysis capabilities
- **Multi-Modal Analysis**: Combine LLM insights with traditional static analysis tools like CodeQL and dynamic analysis with Docker containers

### Target Audience
- Security researchers and penetration testers
- AI/ML researchers studying LLM application in cybersecurity
- Software development teams seeking automated security validation
- Academic institutions conducting security benchmarking studies

## Technical Architecture

AutoPoV follows a sophisticated multi-agent architecture built on LangGraph, combining reactive programming with asynchronous processing to handle complex vulnerability detection workflows.

```mermaid
flowchart TD
    A[Frontend Interface] --> B[FastAPI Backend]
    B --> C[Agent Graph Orchestrator]
    C --> D[Code Ingestion Agent]
    C --> E[CodeQL Analysis Agent]
    C --> F[Investigator Agent]
    C --> G[Verifier Agent]
    C --> H[Docker Runner Agent]
    
    D --> I[Vector Store]
    E --> J[Static Analysis]
    F --> K[LLM Analysis]
    G --> L[PoV Generation]
    H --> M[Docker Environment]
    
    N[CLI Interface] --> B
    O[Webhook Handler] --> B
    P[Report Generator] --> B
    
    I --> K
    J --> F
    K --> L
    L --> M
```

### Component Breakdown

The system is organized into several key architectural layers:

**API Layer**: Built with FastAPI providing RESTful endpoints for scan initiation, status monitoring, and result retrieval
**Agent Layer**: LangGraph-based workflow orchestrator managing the complete vulnerability detection pipeline
**Analysis Layer**: Specialized agents for code ingestion, static analysis, LLM-based investigation, and PoV validation
**Execution Layer**: Docker-based sandboxed environment for safe PoV script execution
**Storage Layer**: Vector database for code context retrieval and persistent scan result storage

### Design Patterns

- **Agent-Based Architecture**: Each vulnerability detection phase operates as an independent agent with specialized responsibilities
- **Pipeline Pattern**: Sequential processing stages with clear data transformation between components
- **Observer Pattern**: Real-time status updates and progress tracking throughout the scanning process
- **Factory Pattern**: Dynamic agent instantiation based on configuration and availability

## Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points

**Backend Application** (`autopov/app/main.py`)
- FastAPI application serving as the central API gateway
- Implements comprehensive scan orchestration and management
- Provides real-time streaming of scan progress via Server-Sent Events
- Supports multiple input sources: Git repositories, ZIP uploads, and direct code paste

**Frontend Application** (`autopov/frontend/src/App.jsx`)
- React-based single-page application with modern UI/UX
- Responsive design using Tailwind CSS and React Router
- Interactive scan forms with validation and progress indicators
- Real-time dashboard for scan monitoring and results visualization

**CLI Interface** (`autopov/cli/autopov.py`)
- Command-line tool for automation and scripting scenarios
- Rich terminal interface with progress bars and formatted output
- Multiple output formats: JSON, table, and PDF reports
- API key management and configuration utilities

### Core Modules

**Configuration Management** (`autopov/app/config.py`)
- Centralized settings management with environment variable support
- Multi-model LLM configuration (OpenAI and Ollama)
- Vector store and embedding model configuration
- Docker and security tool integration settings

**Scan Management** (`autopov/app/scan_manager.py`)
- Thread pool executor for parallel scan processing
- Persistent scan state management with CSV logging
- Metrics collection and historical tracking
- Cost control and resource management

**Agent Graph Orchestration** (`autopov/app/agent_graph.py`)
- LangGraph workflow definition for vulnerability detection pipeline
- State machine implementation with conditional branching
- Integration points for all specialized agents
- Progress tracking and error handling

### Configuration Approach

**Environment Configuration** (`autopov/.env.example`)
- API keys for external services (OpenRouter, GitHub, GitLab)
- Model selection and pricing configuration
- Vector store and database settings
- Docker and security tool paths

**Frontend Configuration** (`autopov/frontend/package.json`)
- React application dependencies and build configuration
- Development server setup and hot-reload capabilities
- UI component libraries and styling frameworks

### External Dependencies

**Core Framework** (`autopov/requirements.txt`)
- FastAPI and Uvicorn for web server functionality
- Pydantic for data validation and configuration management
- LangChain ecosystem for LLM integration and RAG capabilities
- ChromaDB for vector store operations

**Security Analysis Tools**
- CodeQL CLI for static analysis
- Joern for CPG analysis (specifically for use-after-free detection)
- Docker SDK for containerized execution environments

**Development and Testing**
- React ecosystem for frontend development
- Vite for fast build tooling and development server
- PyTest for comprehensive backend testing suite

### Integration Points

**Webhook Integration** (`autopov/app/webhook_handler.py`)
- GitHub and GitLab webhook support for CI/CD pipeline integration
- Automated scan triggering based on repository events
- Security validation through signature verification

**Authentication System** (`autopov/app/auth.py`)
- API key management with role-based access control
- Admin key validation for privileged operations
- Secure token generation and validation

**Report Generation** (`autopov/app/report_generator.py`)
- Multi-format report creation (JSON, PDF)
- Automated documentation generation
- Historical trend analysis and metrics reporting

### Component Relationships

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[FastAPI Main] --> B[Scan Manager]
    B --> C[Agent Graph]
    C --> D[Code Ingestion]
    C --> E[CodeQL Analysis]
    C --> F[Investigator]
    C --> G[Verifier]
    C --> H[Docker Runner]
    
    D --> I[Vector Store]
    E --> J[Static Analysis]
    F --> K[LLM Analysis]
    G --> L[PoV Generation]
    H --> M[Sandbox Execution]
    
    N[Auth System] --> A
    O[Webhook Handler] --> A
    P[Report Generator] --> A
```

## Key Features

### Functionality Overview

**Multi-Source Code Analysis**
- Git repository cloning with branch and commit support
- ZIP file upload processing with automatic extraction
- Direct code paste functionality with language detection
- Local directory scanning with automatic ZIP packaging

**Advanced Vulnerability Detection**
- Support for four major CWE categories: Buffer Overflow (CWE-119), SQL Injection (CWE-89), Use After Free (CWE-416), and Integer Overflow (CWE-190)
- Hybrid analysis combining static analysis tools with LLM-based reasoning
- Context-aware vulnerability investigation with RAG-enhanced code analysis
- Confidence scoring and detailed explanation generation

**Proof-of-Vulnerability Automation**
- Automatic PoV script generation using LLM guidance
- Multi-stage validation process with retry mechanisms
- Safe execution in isolated Docker containers
- Deterministic PoV creation with standard library constraints

### Implementation Highlights

**Real-Time Monitoring** (`autopov/frontend/src/pages/ScanProgress.jsx`)
- Live progress tracking with status updates
- Streamed log output via Server-Sent Events
- Interactive dashboard with filtering and sorting capabilities
- Performance metrics and cost tracking visualization

**Historical Analysis** (`autopov/frontend/src/pages/History.jsx`)
- Comprehensive scan history with filtering options
- Performance trend analysis across multiple scans
- Cost comparison and efficiency metrics
- Export capabilities for audit trails

**Administrative Controls** (`autopov/frontend/src/pages/Settings.jsx`)
- API key management with generation and revocation
- System health monitoring and tool availability checks
- Configuration validation and troubleshooting tools
- User management and access control interfaces

### Feature Architecture

```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> Created
    Created --> Running: Start Scan
    Running --> CodeQL_Analysis: Ingest Complete
    CodeQL_Analysis --> Investigation: Findings Detected
    Investigation --> PoV_Generation: High Confidence
    Investigation --> Skipped: Low Confidence
    PoV_Generation --> Validation: Script Generated
    Validation --> PoV_Execution: Valid Script
    Validation --> PoV_Generation: Invalid Script
    PoV_Execution --> Confirmed: Vulnerability Triggered
    PoV_Execution --> Failed: Execution Error
    PoV_Generation --> Failed: Generation Failed
    Skipped --> Completed: All Findings Processed
    Confirmed --> Completed: All Findings Processed
    Failed --> Completed: All Findings Processed
    Completed --> [*]
```

The AutoPoV framework represents a comprehensive solution for automated vulnerability detection and verification, combining modern AI techniques with established security analysis methodologies. Its modular architecture enables extensibility for additional vulnerability types and analysis tools, while maintaining robust security practices through containerized execution environments.

**Sources:**
- [main.py](autopov/app/main.py)
- [config.py](autopov/app/config.py)
- [investigator.py](autopov/agents/investigator.py)
- [scan_manager.py](autopov/app/scan_manager.py)
- [agent_graph.py](autopov/app/agent_graph.py)
- [prompts.py](autopov/prompts.py)
- [package.json](autopov/frontend/package.json)
- [requirements.txt](autopov/requirements.txt)
- [App.jsx](autopov/frontend/src/App.jsx)
- [Home.jsx](autopov/frontend/src/pages/Home.jsx)
- [autopov.py](autopov/cli/autopov.py)
- [docker_runner.py](autopov/agents/docker_runner.py)

</blog>
